title: Merck, Eisai’s Keytruda triplet fails to improve survival in kidney cancer
snippet: A triplet regimen comprising Merck’s Welireg and PD-1 blockbuster Keytruda and Eisai’s Lenvima flopped in a Phase 3 renal cell carcinoma trial.

title: AACR 2026: Moderna, Revolution, Zymeworks and BeOne showcase new data
snippet: Moderna, Revolution Medicines and Zymeworks record wins in melanoma, lung and breast cancers, while BeOne swings and misses at head and neck cancer.

title: Gilead, Arcus tumble again in TIGIT, triggering 2 trial culls
snippet: The Phase 3 failure has prompted Gilead Sciences and Arcus Biosciences to terminate a mid-stage study of their TIGIT asset in lung cancer.

title: As Duchenne innovation booms, let’s ensure it becomes real progress
snippet: At Sarepta Therapeutics, we’ve seen it all. Here are the questions I believe we should be asking to move forward in Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

title: Viatris recalls extended-release Xanax over dissolution test failure
snippet: Viatris, which Pfizer created in 2020, voluntarily withdrew extended-release products made at a plant in Ireland after an analysis revealed an issue that could affect bioavailability.

title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

title: Lilly adds gene delivery technology to CAR T in up to $7B Kelonia deal
snippet: After entering the CAR T space in February, Eli Lilly is “jumping into in vivo CAR-T with both feet” with the acquisition of Kelonia Therapeutics and its gene delivery technology.

title: Agenus cancer cocktail records 0% response rate, missing midstage goal
snippet: An investigational cocktail was tied to a 0% overall response rate in patients with gastroesophageal cancer, but developers Agenus and MiNK Therapeutics aren’t giving up on the program just yet.

title: Novo’s late-stage sickle cell win piles pressure on competitors
snippet: Novo Nordisk’s etavopivat elicited a 27% drop in vaso-occlusive crises and 48.7% hemoglobin response after 24 weeks, creating “separation amongst PK class candidates,” Truist analysts said on Monday. Novo plans to seek FDA approval in the back half of 2026.

title: FDA Policy Tracker 2026: Psychedelics get a boost
snippet: A year of significant policy change at the FDA brought momentum and scrutiny into the new year. As 2026 gets underway, biopharma companies are responding to sweeping vaccine changes while concerns surface about the politicization of the agency.

title: Odyssey tests IPO waters anew after quitting quest last year
snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

title: BMS layoffs hits Lawrenceville hard, adding 200+ to cut count
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Merck’s early PD-1/VEGF data competitive in lung cancer, but Summit ‘looms large’
snippet: While Merck’s PD-1/VEGF asset appears to match the performance of Summit Therapeutics’ ivonescimab, the pharma’s Phase 1/2 readout in non-small cell lung cancer still leaves analysts with some questions moving into later-stage development.

title: UCB elevates epilepsy expertise with up to $1.15B Neurona takeover
snippet: The acquisition of Neurona will put UCB in both the epilepsy and cell therapy space, even as many of its fellow pharmas move away from the latter modality.

title: Trump throws support behind psychedelics with executive order
snippet: A new executive order could usher in psychedelics as the “key next wave” of mental health therapies, according to analysts at RBC Capital Markets.

title: From Product to Patient in Nuclear Medicine: Why Vertical Integration Is Essential for a Competitive Advantage
snippet: In nuclear medicine, innovation alone isn’t enough. What matters is whether it can be delivered on time, every time, because timing is critical for our patients. And that delivery promise must hold up under real-world pressure – from routine variability to sudden disruptions that can reroute global logistics overnight.

title: Lilly’s Foundayo reaches 1,390 patients in first week, trailing Novo’s oral Wegovy launch
snippet: While Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill reached more than 3,000 patients in its first week on the market, analysts at RBC Capital Markets said a direct comparison of the two figures could be misleading given the shorter data collection time for Foundayo.

title: UPDATE: Kailera breaks biotech IPO record with $625M debut
snippet: Obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics debuted on the Nasdaq Friday after raising a record $625 million, beating Moderna’s $600 million from 2018.

title: RFK Jr. defends Makary, claims pharma ‘owns’ Congress and media
snippet: The pharma industry “own Congress, they own the media,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. told lawmakers by way of explaining the bad press against FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the second rejection of Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug.

title: Novo may have muscle advantage over Lilly in weight-loss race: preprint
snippet: More patients on Eli Lilly’s tirzepatide lost over 5% of their lean mass versus those on Novo Nordisk’s semaglutide, according to a study that has yet to be peer reviewed.

title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

title: With oral obesity race underway, all eyes are on Lilly’s Foundayo launch
snippet: Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy has a few months’ head start on Eli Lilly’s newly approved pill. While the Indianapolis pharma has come from behind the Danish rival in the weight loss space before, last time it clearly had the better drug.

title: Pharma's Reluctance To Serve Europe To Dodge MFN Drug Pricing Is Chilling
snippet: As Big Pharma companies consider foregoing European drug launches to avoid reducing drug prices in the U.S. in alignment with Trump’s Most Favored Nation policy, patients will suffer.
